name: pcs-promo-creation
description: Guided end-to-end PCS promo pipeline: parse a vendor deck -> build NetSuite kit imports -> create Jira promotion tasks, with a Yes/No confirmation before each stage and the existing hard PROM write-protection at the Jira step. Orchestrates pcs-promo-parser and pcs-jira-task-builder and drives the Kit Builder `kb.exe` CLI binary (>= 0.5.22); Claude writes each kit's Page Title (95-char cap, every member SKU kept, 'Kit' filler dropped) and Detailed Description from the item data using the ported title/description rules. v1.13.0: when the NS export shows SKUs not yet built in NetSuite, the kit stage captures a resume kit — the unbuilt-SKU list, an unbuilt DECODE (copy-paste artifact), and a separate Unbuilt-SKU Promo-List of just the affected kits, plus a README — saved to the session folder so a later/separate chat can pick those kits up once the items are built. v1.12.0: the NetSuite kit stage surfaces the Promo Kit Support saved-search link (account 855722, search 16021) in both the Step 3 chat text and the DECODE copy-paste artifact, so the operator clicks straight through to run the search. v1.11.0: token discovery is robust — it finds the GitHub token in a subfolder or oddly-named file and extracts github_pat_/ghp_ by shape instead of assuming a GITHUB_TOKEN= line, and the non-Windows (Cowork Linux sandbox) source install is fixed (token-injected pip URL, drop winget). v1.10.0: Step 0 settles the Kit Builder up front — if `kb.exe` is missing and a `.env` token is present it installs it before parsing (no longer deferred to the kit stage); if no token is found it tells the operator to get the GitHub token `.env` from their admin and offers to continue without kit building (deck parsing + Jira still run; the NetSuite kit stages are skipped). v1.9.0: the Kit Builder now ships as a prebuilt headless `kb.exe` — Step 0 checks `kb.exe --version` (>= 0.5.22) and, on Windows, Claude fetches `kb.exe` from the private Release via the `.env` token (no Python/pip/Git); source `pip install` stays as the dev/non-Windows fallback. v1.8.0: Stage-1 validation enforces the parser's new verification gate — it re-matches every emitted SKU to the vendor regex, asserts no `unverified`-held SKU leaked into an output, reconciles `SKUs Held`, and surfaces verification-held items (blocked from the Promo-List) at Gate 2. v1.7.0: requires kb >= 0.5.21 (which collapses duplicate kit members that share an Item ID); the Step 0 prereq check uses `pip show pcs-kit-builder-lite` (the CLI has no `kb --version`). v1.6.0: the DECODE formula is presented as a copy-paste artifact in chat (one Copy button per block), alongside the backup decode_blocks.txt. v1.5.0: a run now lands in one session folder `Parsed Decks/<Vendor>/<Vendor>-<QN>-<YYYY>-<MM-DD>/` with three subfolders — Promo Parsed Output / NetSuite Import Files / Images; the drag-and-drop upload widget is recreated on every file (re)prompt; the parser's For-Review workbook is surfaced at the kit gate; the kit stage is skipped when there are no kit promos. v1.4.0: kit images are composed locally via a `kb --images-only` one-liner (macOS + Windows) since Cowork's sandbox can't reach NetSuite's image host. Prompts for file uploads (deck PDF, pricing cheat sheet, NetSuite Promo Kit Support export) when needed.
